question: What is the distribution of users by organization and their learning activity status?
sql: SELECT o.name as organization_name, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as total_users, COUNT(CASE WHEN p.active = 'Y' THEN 1 END) as active_users, COUNT(CASE WHEN p.deleted = 'N' THEN 1 END) as non_deleted_users, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN p.active = 'Y' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk), 2) as active_percentage FROM person_sumtotal p JOIN personorganization_sumtotal po ON p.personpk = po.personfk JOIN organization_sumtotal o ON po.organizationfk = o.organizationpk GROUP BY o.name ORDER BY total_users DESC
context: Direct Visualization - Bar Chart. Shows user distribution across organizations with activity status for organizational learning analytics.
instructions: Analyze user distribution by organization to understand learning platform adoption and engagement across different business units.

question: Which users have the highest number of learning attempts and what are their completion rates?
sql: SELECT p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.success = 'true' THEN 1 END) as successful_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score FROM person_sumtotal p JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk WHERE p.active = 'Y' GROUP BY p.personpk, p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname HAVING COUNT(a.attempt_pk) >= 5 ORDER BY total_attempts DESC, completion_rate DESC LIMIT 20
context: Direct Visualization - Top N. Identifies most active learners with performance metrics for learning analytics and recognition programs.
instructions: Rank users by learning activity and performance to identify top performers and learning engagement patterns.

question: What are the most popular learning activities and their success rates?
sql: SELECT act.activityname as activity_name, act.code as activity_code, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.success = 'true' THEN 1 END) as successful_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.success = 'true' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as success_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityname, act.code ORDER BY total_attempts DESC LIMIT 15
context: Direct Visualization - Bar Chart. Shows course popularity and effectiveness metrics for curriculum management and optimization.
instructions: Analyze course popularity and success rates to optimize curriculum and identify high-performing learning content.

question: How has learning activity participation changed over time by month?
sql: SELECT DATE_FORMAT(a.startdt, '%Y-%m') as learning_month, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_learners, COUNT(DISTINCT a.activityfk) as unique_activities, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a WHERE a.startdt >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 12 MONTH) GROUP BY DATE_FORMAT(a.startdt, '%Y-%m') ORDER BY learning_month
context: Direct Visualization - Line Chart. Shows monthly learning trends for understanding seasonal patterns and platform growth.
instructions: Track monthly learning participation trends to understand seasonal patterns and platform adoption growth.

question: Which organizations have the highest learning engagement and completion rates?
sql: SELECT o.name as organization_name, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as total_users, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(a.attempt_pk) / COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk), 2) as avg_attempts_per_user, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM organization_sumtotal o JOIN personorganization_sumtotal po ON o.organizationpk = po.organizationfk JOIN person_sumtotal p ON po.personfk = p.personpk LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk WHERE o.deleted = 'N' AND p.active = 'Y' GROUP BY o.organizationpk, o.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) >= 10 ORDER BY completion_rate DESC, avg_attempts_per_user DESC
context: Direct Visualization - Bar Chart. Shows organizational learning performance for identifying best practices and improvement opportunities.
instructions: Compare organizational learning performance to identify best practices and areas for improvement across business units.

question: What is the average time spent on learning activities by activity type?
sql: SELECT act.activityname as activity_name, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.elapsedseconds AS DECIMAL) / 60), 2) as avg_minutes_spent, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.elapsedseconds AS DECIMAL) / 3600), 2) as avg_hours_spent, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE a.elapsedseconds IS NOT NULL AND a.elapsedseconds > 0 GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityname ORDER BY avg_hours_spent DESC
context: Maths/Analytics - Learning Duration Analysis. Shows time investment patterns for curriculum planning and learner experience optimization.
instructions: Analyze learning duration patterns to optimize course design and understand learner time investment across different activities.

question: Which users have not completed any learning activities in the last 90 days?
sql: SELECT p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname, p.createddate, MAX(a.enddt) as last_activity_date, DATEDIFF(CURRENT_DATE, MAX(a.enddt)) as days_since_last_activity FROM person_sumtotal p LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND p.deleted = 'N' GROUP BY p.personpk, p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname, p.createddate HAVING MAX(a.enddt) IS NULL OR DATEDIFF(CURRENT_DATE, MAX(a.enddt)) > 90 ORDER BY days_since_last_activity DESC
context: Direct Visualization - Table. Identifies inactive learners requiring re-engagement strategies and follow-up.
instructions: Identify inactive learners to develop re-engagement strategies and improve platform adoption.

question: What are the most challenging learning activities based on low completion rates?
sql: SELECT act.activityname as activity_name, act.code as activity_code, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.success = 'false' THEN 1 END) as failed_attempts FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityname, act.code HAVING COUNT(a.attempt_pk) >= 10 ORDER BY completion_rate ASC, avg_score ASC LIMIT 15
context: Direct Visualization - Bar Chart. Identifies challenging courses requiring content review and instructional design improvements.
instructions: Identify challenging courses with low completion rates to prioritize content review and instructional design improvements.

question: What is the learning progress distribution by user demographics (gender, company)?
sql: SELECT p.gendercode, p.companyname, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as total_users, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score FROM person_sumtotal p LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND p.deleted = 'N' GROUP BY p.gendercode, p.companyname HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) >= 5 ORDER BY completion_rate DESC
context: Direct Visualization - Stacked Bar Chart. Shows learning performance by demographics for diversity and inclusion analysis.
instructions: Analyze learning performance by demographics to ensure equitable learning opportunities and identify potential barriers.

question: Which learning activities require registration and how does this affect participation?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN act.openforreg = 'Y' THEN 'Registration Required' ELSE 'No Registration Required' END as registration_status, COUNT(act.activity_pk) as total_activities, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_participants, ROUND(COUNT(a.attempt_pk) / COUNT(act.activity_pk), 2) as avg_attempts_per_activity, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' GROUP BY act.openforreg ORDER BY avg_attempts_per_activity DESC
context: Direct Visualization - Column Chart. Compares participation patterns between registration-required and open activities.
instructions: Analyze the impact of registration requirements on learning participation to optimize course accessibility.

question: What is the average number of attempts per user for each learning activity?
sql: SELECT act.activityname as activity_name, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_learners, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(a.attempt_pk) / COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk), 2) as avg_attempts_per_learner, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.currentattemptind = 'Y' THEN 1 END) as current_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityname ORDER BY avg_attempts_per_learner DESC
context: Maths/Analytics - Attempt Pattern Analysis. Shows retry patterns and learning persistence for course difficulty assessment.
instructions: Analyze attempt patterns to understand course difficulty and learner persistence across different activities.

question: Which users have the highest learning scores and what activities did they complete?
sql: SELECT p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score, ROUND(MAX(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as max_score, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.success = 'true' THEN 1 END) as successful_attempts, GROUP_CONCAT(DISTINCT act.activityname ORDER BY act.activityname SEPARATOR ', ') as completed_activities FROM person_sumtotal p JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk JOIN tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act ON a.activityfk = act.activity_pk WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND a.score IS NOT NULL AND a.success = 'true' GROUP BY p.personpk, p.firstname, p.lastname, p.companyname HAVING COUNT(a.attempt_pk) >= 3 ORDER BY avg_score DESC, total_attempts DESC LIMIT 15
context: Direct Visualization - Top N. Identifies top-performing learners and their completed activities for recognition programs.
instructions: Identify top-performing learners and their achievements to support recognition programs and best practice sharing.

question: What is the distribution of learning activities by activity label and their performance?
sql: SELECT al.labelname as activity_label, COUNT(act.activity_pk) as total_activities, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_learners, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score FROM actlabel_sumtotal al JOIN tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act ON al.actlabelpk = act.activitylabelfk LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' GROUP BY al.actlabelpk, al.labelname ORDER BY total_attempts DESC
context: Direct Visualization - Bar Chart. Shows learning performance by activity category for curriculum planning and optimization.
instructions: Analyze learning performance by activity labels to understand category effectiveness and optimize curriculum structure.

question: How many users are currently enrolled in learning activities and what is their progress status?
sql: SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as enrolled_users, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'In Progress' THEN 1 END) as in_progress_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) as completed_attempts, COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Not Started' THEN 1 END) as not_started_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a WHERE a.currentattemptind = 'Y'
context: Dashboard Widget - Current Learning Status. Shows real-time learning progress for monitoring active learners.
instructions: Monitor current learning status to track active learners and their progress across the platform.

question: Which organizations have the most diverse learning activity participation?
sql: SELECT o.name as organization_name, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as total_users, COUNT(DISTINCT a.activityfk) as unique_activities, COUNT(DISTINCT act.activityname) as unique_activity_names, ROUND(COUNT(DISTINCT a.activityfk) / COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk), 2) as activities_per_user, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts FROM organization_sumtotal o JOIN personorganization_sumtotal po ON o.organizationpk = po.organizationfk JOIN person_sumtotal p ON po.personfk = p.personpk LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act ON a.activityfk = act.activity_pk WHERE o.deleted = 'N' AND p.active = 'Y' GROUP BY o.organizationpk, o.name HAVING COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) >= 10 ORDER BY unique_activities DESC, activities_per_user DESC
context: Direct Visualization - Bar Chart. Shows organizational learning diversity for identifying comprehensive learning cultures.
instructions: Analyze organizational learning diversity to identify comprehensive learning cultures and knowledge sharing patterns.

question: What is the correlation between learning duration and success rates across different activities?
sql: SELECT act.activityname as activity_name, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.elapsedseconds AS DECIMAL) / 60), 2) as avg_minutes, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.success = 'true' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as success_rate, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_score, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE a.elapsedseconds IS NOT NULL AND a.elapsedseconds > 0 GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityname HAVING COUNT(a.attempt_pk) >= 10 ORDER BY success_rate DESC, avg_minutes ASC
context: Maths/Analytics - Duration vs Success Analysis. Shows relationship between learning time investment and success rates.
instructions: Analyze the relationship between learning duration and success rates to optimize course design and time allocation.

question: Which learning activities have the highest launch counts and what does this indicate about popularity?
sql: SELECT act.activityname as activity_name, act.code as activity_code, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.launchcount AS DECIMAL)), 2) as avg_launch_count, ROUND(MAX(CAST(a.launchcount AS DECIMAL)), 2) as max_launch_count, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_learners, ROUND(COUNT(a.attempt_pk) / COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk), 2) as attempts_per_learner FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON act.activity_pk = a.activityfk WHERE act.active = 'Y' AND a.launchcount IS NOT NULL GROUP BY act.activity_pk, act.activityname, act.code ORDER BY avg_launch_count DESC, total_attempts DESC LIMIT 15
context: Direct Visualization - Bar Chart. Shows course popularity based on launch frequency for content prioritization.
instructions: Analyze course popularity based on launch frequency to prioritize content development and resource allocation.

question: What is the learning activity participation pattern for users by their start date?
sql: SELECT CASE WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY) THEN 'New (30 days)' WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 90 DAY) THEN 'Recent (90 days)' WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 365 DAY) THEN 'Mature (1 year)' ELSE 'Established (1+ years)' END as user_tenure, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as total_users, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, ROUND(COUNT(a.attempt_pk) / COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk), 2) as avg_attempts_per_user, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM person_sumtotal p LEFT JOIN tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a ON p.personpk = a.empfk WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND p.deleted = 'N' GROUP BY CASE WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY) THEN 'New (30 days)' WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 90 DAY) THEN 'Recent (90 days)' WHEN p.startdate >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 365 DAY) THEN 'Mature (1 year)' ELSE 'Established (1+ years)' END ORDER BY total_users DESC
context: Direct Visualization - Stacked Bar Chart. Shows learning participation patterns by user tenure for onboarding analysis.
instructions: Analyze learning participation by user tenure to understand onboarding effectiveness and long-term engagement patterns.

question: What are the peak learning hours and days based on activity start times?
sql: SELECT HOUR(a.startdt) as learning_hour, DAYNAME(a.startdt) as learning_day, COUNT(a.attempt_pk) as total_attempts, COUNT(DISTINCT a.empfk) as unique_learners, ROUND(COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) * 100.0 / COUNT(a.attempt_pk), 2) as completion_rate FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a WHERE a.startdt >= DATE_SUB(CURRENT_DATE, INTERVAL 30 DAY) GROUP BY HOUR(a.startdt), DAYNAME(a.startdt) ORDER BY total_attempts DESC
context: Direct Visualization - Heatmap. Shows learning activity patterns by time and day for optimal scheduling recommendations.
instructions: Analyze learning time patterns to optimize course scheduling and identify peak learning periods.

question: Create a comprehensive learning dashboard showing key metrics across all SumTotal Learn data.
sql: SELECT 'Total Users' as metric, COUNT(DISTINCT p.personpk) as value FROM person_sumtotal p WHERE p.active = 'Y' AND p.deleted = 'N' UNION ALL SELECT 'Active Organizations', COUNT(DISTINCT o.organizationpk) FROM organization_sumtotal o WHERE o.deleted = 'N' UNION ALL SELECT 'Learning Activities', COUNT(DISTINCT act.activity_pk) FROM tbl_tmx_activity_sumtotal act WHERE act.active = 'Y' UNION ALL SELECT 'Total Learning Attempts', COUNT(a.attempt_pk) FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a UNION ALL SELECT 'Completed Attempts', COUNT(CASE WHEN a.completionstatus = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a UNION ALL SELECT 'Successful Attempts', COUNT(CASE WHEN a.success = 'true' THEN 1 END) FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a UNION ALL SELECT 'Average Score', ROUND(AVG(CAST(a.score AS DECIMAL)), 2) FROM tbl_tmx_attempt_sumtotal a WHERE a.score IS NOT NULL
context: Dashboard Widget - Learning Platform Overview. Comprehensive learning metrics for executive dashboard and platform assessment.
instructions: Calculate comprehensive learning platform metrics across all models for executive-level platform assessment and reporting.
